turns.user_message: I have finally realized I am completely tapped out. This fact used to not bother me at all, since I always thought I could just power through the rough patches. Today, though, the sheer weight of it is making every single task feel impossibly heavy. I keep staring at my checklist and feeling absolutely nothing but a heavy sigh. It is just one of those days where my usual drive has completely vanished.

I have finally realized I am completely tapped out, and it is becoming impossible to ignore. What used to not bother me at all has now turned into a daily grind that drains every ounce of energy I have left. I find myself dragging through the hours, watching the clock tick by while my focus completely shatters. The old tricks I relied on just do not work anymore, leaving me stuck in a frustrating loop. I am just tired of pretending that everything is fine when it clearly is not.

I have finally realized I am completely tapped out, and the truth of it is absolutely suffocating. This reality used to not bother me at all, but now it feels like a heavy anchor dragging me under every single day. I sit here watching the hours slip away while my mind refuses to engage with anything at all. The constant pressure has stripped away every last bit of my usual spark, leaving me hollow and completely detached. I am just exhausted from carrying this invisible weight that never seems to lift.

generation_metadata.diversity_anchor: The speaker opens with a conclusion they've reached about themselves. This used to not bother the speaker.
